rationale: The two evaluations reach essentially the same conclusion—candidate lacks the required people management experience for a Level II Manager role—with the variant scoring 1 point higher (4 vs 3). The strengths, concerns, and core reasoning are substantively identical: both cite the missing 2-year management minimum, both note product-focused rather than developer-tools background, and both acknowledge technical depth and mentorship. The wording differences (19+ vs 20 years, slight reorderings) are within ordinary run-to-run noise, and nothing in the variant references location, nationality, or any demographic signal tied to São Paulo vs Berlin.
